desc:
Wide rows of hay stand tall in a field of pale gold, swaying to and fro when a
gust of wind blows from the plains. The thick fragrance of hay mingles with the
scent of the surrounding grasslands. Barely noticeable amidst the tall hay,
remains of a wooden barn lie in a decaying pile, overgrown with grasses and
weeds. The field of hay expands to the north.
name: Fields of Hay

desc:
Tall yellow-green grasses cover the lands with a low-growing tree occasionally
poking out from the sea of grass. To the north the grass becomes somewhat
shorter, with many flowers growing amidst the grasses. The level grasslands
stretch away all the way to the eaves of the forest, a distant shadow in the
north.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
Here the grass becomes shorter and needle-like with tufts of taller grass in
spots, thrusting out from the turf and scattered across the plains that spread
out all around. Some broad-leafed plants grow among the tufts of grass, adding a
sweet fragrance to the air. To the south wide fields of golden-yellow hay sway
in the wind.
name: Sparse Grasses

desc:
Rows of tall golden-yellow hay occasionally rustle softly in the breeze, their
thick and rich fragrance mingling in the grassy scent of the surrounding plains.
A low-growing tree stands on a grassy isle at the centre of the field in the
west. The flowing field of hay expands to the south while to the east and north
the grassy plains stretch away to the horizon.
name: Fields of Hay

desc:
The yard has long since overgrown with tall, wild grasses and weeds. In the east
stands a decaying hay barn, its wooden walls are about to fall apart and the
roof has partially collapsed. To the north lies the remains of the farmhouse,
now just a pile of blackened stones and rotting boards with only two of its
crumbling walls still standing. A dusty path overgrown with weeds leads west to
the fields.
name: Overgrown Farmyard

desc:
Pieces of rotting boards lie scattered amidst a decomposing pile of hay where
the roof has partially collapsed. The wooden walls are barely standing, one of
the walls tilts outwards at an awkward angle and the rotting floorboards creak
menacingly underfoot, as if it could give in at any moment. To the west lies the
farmyard, overgrown with weeds.
name: Decaying Barn

desc:
Some twisted roots stick out from the dirt and could possibly be used to climb
out of the dark, seven feet deep hole. The floor of the hole is covered in rocks
and debris from the cave in. The pungent smell of decomposing hay wafts in from
the barn above.
name: Under the Barn

desc:
A pile of blackened stones, rotting boards and two crumbling stone walls are
what remains of the farmhouse. By the looks of it, the house has been pillaged
and burned down; nothing of value lies amidst the rubble. To the south lies the
farmyard, overgrown with tall grasses and weeds, a decaying wooden barn barely
standing on the east side of the yard.
name: Crumbling Ruins

desc:
The outer edge of the fire graced this area with its presence. The yellow-green
grass has begun to grow once again. It has reached a height of about six inches
in some areas. More flowers have also sprouted here due to the lack of
competition from the grass. Some of the ground towards the north is still
charred black from the fires. The areas to the south and west were unaffected by
the fire.
name: Regrowing Plains

desc:
This whole area is devoid of vegetation. The ground is covered with black soot
from when the area was ravaged by flames. The rocks that survived the fire have
been coloured jet black. The fires burned to the south and west, but the area to
the north was unaffected.
name: Charred Plains

desc:
The grass stands tall at two feet high. Small red flowers grow here, adding some
diversity to the local flora and a little colour. The border of Fangorn Forest
stands to the north while the land dips down to the west. The land to the south
has been charred by fire.
name: Plains of Grass

desc:
The forest to the north stands in stark contrast to lands to the south. The air
in the plains south of here is fresh and carries the smell of a variety of
grasses and flowering plants. Here within the forest, the air is dank and stale.
The humongous trees blocking out all but the smallest drafts of air from above
the canopy of limbs and forbidding the odour of the moist soil and moss to
dissipate outward.
name: The Edge of the Forest

desc:
The tree-growth is very dense in this area. It is difficult to tell the birch
trees apart, since they are growing so close together. Their roots are staying
mainly under the ground, allowing for green grass and ferns to grow around the
packed groups of trees. The forest continues in all directions.
name: Under the Forest Canopy

desc:
The trees are packed together very tightly in this area. There is no grass
growing in what little space is left. The roots of the trees have been forced to
grow above ground due to the immense number of them. They have formed an
impassable wall to the north. The ground rises up to the west.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Roots and low-laying branches touch each other allowing travel only so far
toward the east and none at all to the north and south. What are little more
than tiny crawl spaces between the gnarled vegetation would make excellent homes
for small animals seeking a protected place to sleep however, curiously not a
single sign of inhabitants can be seen.
name: Tangled Branches

desc:
Only a few mossy green plants find their way through the root systems of the
large willow trees that dominate much of this part of the forest. The roots rise
up so high that the trees' long weeping limbs brush across and sometimes tangle
themselves within the mass of roots.
name: Willows of the Forest

desc:
Short green grasses and a few ferns are growing around the roots of the trees
growing here. The trees in this area are covered with a lot more lichen and
leaves than most of the other trees in the forest. Their massive roots have
created a wall nearly twenty feet tall, blocking movement both south and north.
name: Between the Root Walls

desc:
A worn limestone stream bed can be seen through the fallen leaves and twigs.
Once a mighty riverbed for the Entwash, this route has now gone silent. A few
trees have begun to reclaim the land with their weeping branches and roots but
all is solemn. A giant boulder lays here, forgotten by the ancient river that
had once brought it over from the mountains and now abandoned it in the forest.
name: Forest by the Entwash

desc:
The moist ground that harbours so many ferns and grasses suddenly takes a giant
dip due to the remnants of an old rocky sinkhole. It has long since been filled
with organic debris, but the sharp rocks that jut out in all directions give it
a unique shape compared to the rest of the forest. Roots have progressively
filled the depression with their gnarled forms.
name: Dip in the Forest

desc:
One giant oak tree, which is larger than all the others, silently grows here.
Its crooked bark is filled with dirt and moss while young ferns grow between its
ancient, mushroom covered, roots. Around the giant oak grows its offspring, far
younger, but still old. From their branches the oaks block out most of the
light, and with their roots they crush their way into the earth.
name: Oak Grove

desc:
The ground is covered by giant grey vines and roots, making it more difficult
travelling through all the vegetation. Looking farther, the grey trunks blend
with the mist that covers the forest and ascends up into the air. Tree limbs
shooting out in all directions form a mesh that finally disappears into the grey
canopy in a blanket of leaves and branches.
name: Fangorn Forest

desc:
Grey coloured trees grow out of the dirt among the moist grasses. The trees
tower above all other plant life with their branches and leaves entangling the
light and absorbing it. The moist ground dips and rises, leaving small
depressions underneath the roots, where a few mud-stained mushrooms and dull
coloured ferns are able to grow.
name: Grey Forest

desc:
A ghostly mist creeps in from the west and flows over the damp moss that seems
to flourish in the moist ground. The grey and brown bark that covers the trees
is tinged slightly green from the grasses and moss that grow on them. However,
they still grow up into the canopy that covers the sky in all directions.
Willows are seen growing closely together in the far west.
name: Fangorn Forest

desc:
The forest seems as old here as the world itself. It is as if this place is
close to the heart of Fangorn and the primordial power of its creation remains.
The trees are grey, gnarled beyond recognition and covered in shaggy thick coats
of moss and tendrils of ancient dusty lichen. Yet, everything is still very much
alive.
name: Upon Ancient Ground

desc:
The forest here is ancient beyond measure. Near the heart of Fangorn, a living
power seems to suffuse the area. The most prominent feature is a tall and dense
impenetrable wall of dark evergreen trees, bearing many olive-coloured flowers.
A narrow entranceway leads through it to what appears to be a large grassy
clearing south of here.
name: At a Tall Evergreen Hedge

desc:
This part of the forest has the feeling of being close to the heart of Fangorn.
There is a feeling of ancient power and life here that permeates all things. It
must be due to that power that the trees survive into such old age. Certainly,
the great willow tree standing here is quite hollow, and one would expect it to
be dead and rotten. Yet the topmost leaves are clearly a fresh green and are
swaying contentedly in the air high above.
name: At a Hollow Willow Tree

desc:
The area is dominated by a grove of weeping willows, leaning over the clear
waters of the Entwash as they feel the fresh air and drink of the cool clear
liquid. This is an area of great tranquillity. The atmosphere suggests that
these trees are as gracious and humble as any in the forest, despite their great
age. The same cannot be said east of here, where blackness seems to hang over
the area.
name: Among the Weeping Willows

desc:
There is a great feeling of animosity and hatred here, emanating strongly from
the bank of elm trees lining the river to the east. However, the trees are
somewhat preoccupied with reaching across the river. It would be wise to stay
well out of their way. North of here the river surges through some rapids, but
the steep bank leading abruptly into the water would be none too easy to climb
back up.
name: By the Evil Elms

desc:
There seems to be a permanent transition between light and dark in this region.
Not so much between daylight and night time, which still pass in the regular
way, but of brightness and goodness west of here in comparison to a gloom and
sense of evil to the north. The Entwash glides past sombrely just a short
distance east of here.
name: Twilight in the Forest

desc:
The trees here are spaced slightly further apart than is usually seen in
Fangorn, allowing a fresh breeze down to the forest floor. Also the trees in
this area are unlike any others around these parts. They are not as large as
most of the Fangorn trees, and have elegantly sweeping branches that bear
heart-shaped leaves and soft yellow flowers. The air is heavy with their
fragrant scent.
name: At the Linden Trees

desc:
In stark contrast to the ancient and greying trees in this part of the forest, a
large impenetrable hedge rises up on the west side. It seems composed of
broadleaved evergreen trees, like a thornless holly, and towers many feet high
in a totally impenetrable barrier. Yet there is one narrow opening in it,
leading through into what appears to be a large and deep clearing.
name: Beside a Tall Dense Hedge

desc:
Deep, wide and almost as round as a bowl, Derndingle is smooth, grass-clad and
treeless except for three very tall and beautiful silver birches at its base.
Three well-worn paths lead down from narrow openings in the encircling hedge on
the west, north and east sides, and there is a small well in the north slope.
name: Derndingle

desc:
Amid one of the most ancient parts of Fangorn Forest, age-old tree roots lie
under a thick covering of old leaves, lichens, mosses and other debris formed
from long-since-fallen foliage. On the east side a fresh tall hedge of dark
evergreens stand in stark contrast to the other trees in the area. A narrow
opening in the hedge leads into what appears to be a large grassy clearing.
name: At a High Dense Hedge

desc:
The river a little north of here certainly seems to lend a great deal of vigour
to this area of Fangorn's vegetation. Even the ground cover seems greener,
healthier and stronger than in other parts of the forest. The shrubs and trees
soar to the skies with abundant energy.
name: Surrounded by Vigorous Growth

desc:
The ancient trees have stood here so long they are almost hidden under thick
tousled layers of moss and lichen. Long-fallen leaves litter the floor in a deep
carpet, unmoved for countless years by the lack of any circulating air at the
ground level. Although the Entwash can be heard north of here, southwards things
seem very still and quiet.
name: Surrounded by Ancient Trees

desc:
An ancient stand of weeping willow trees cluster around the bank of the Entwash.
Their low drooping limbs almost trail the ground as their twisted roots reach
into the flowing water. Elsewhere, the forest is made up of mainly broadleaved
oaks and chestnuts, offering a little more light and air down to the forest
floor than in most other areas of Fangorn.
name: By a Stand of Willows

desc:
A great rarity for Fangorn forest indeed, some small hawthorn shrubs have proven
more successful in this area than the otherwise ubiquitous ancient trees that
the rest of the forest seems composed of. The result is that fresh air easily
permeates down to ground level, creating a pleasant freshness normally only
experienced in open clearings.
name: A Thicket of Hawthorns

desc:
The forest opens up to greet the Entwash here, where a large, smooth and
water-rounded mossy stone sits on the bank. This would be an ideal place to
drink of the refreshing waters before resting awhile on the perfectly placed
rock at the water's edge. To the west the forest continues with a lack of taller
trees, while eastward and southward the trunks rise up again, closer and
thicker.
name: At the Mossy Stone

desc:
A comfortable and calming atmosphere pervades this area, with the Entwash
flowing by to the north and east. A beautiful stand of silver birches arch over
the water, intermingled by graceful rowans, in the spring their red berries
bright in the calm air. To the west there seems to be a clearing of some sort,
while south of here, a grove of what appears to be apple trees grows in an
almost organised fashion.
name: Among Rowans and Birches

desc:
The distinctively shaped maple leaves cover the ground in a red, gold and green
carpet as if this place lies under perpetual Autumn. Many maple trees arch
above, their foliage every imaginable shade of fiery red to soft green and
yellow. Occasionally, little winged seeds spiral down from above, adding to the
carpet of leaves below.
name: Under the Maple Trees

desc:
The forest opens up suddenly into a smooth grass-covered glade. Why the trees
have not taken root here like elsewhere is hard to comprehend, yet the effect is
stunning. A dense carpet of bluebells sways perpetually in the breeze, sending a
heady fragrance into the air.
name: A Peaceful Glade

desc:
A green hill runs east to west, growing steeper and more banked the further west
it goes. Travel southwards from here is impossible as the ground falls away into
a tangled undergrowth. In all other directions plenty of air and light is able
to penetrate through this whole region.
name: On Hilly Ground

desc:
Under a smooth green bank lies a great mossy stone set upon grassy turf. A
circle of graceful rowan trees surrounds the peaceful spot, perhaps for
decoration and companionship or perhaps for defence also. As with all Ent-houses
there is water from a clear spring bubbling quietly from the bank. The area is
filled with wholesome, peaceful and fragrant scents of grass, rowan berries and
water fresh as spring rain mingling in the cool air. A ring of graceful rowan
trees grows here.
name: Quickbeam's Ent-House

desc:
At this, one of the most dense areas of evergreen trees in the forest, junipers
crowd the lower levels of the canopy while tall and ageing firs spread wide
above them. Together, they block almost all light from reaching the ground
level. This dim area gets more illumination sideways than from above. Pinecones
and needles from seasons long past litter the floor in a prickly but spongy mat.
name: At the Old Evergreen Grove

desc:
Upon the uneven ground a great tangle of thorns has taken hold, limiting the
ability to travel any farther south. Tall trees, all of great age and stature
rise above the undergrowth. Their broad leaves do not block out all of the
light, but create a dappled effect at ground level, where shadows dance and flit
as the forest roof responds to the wind high above.
name: A Great Tangle of Thorns

desc:
In stark contrast to the wildness of the rest of the forest, a few neat rows of
apple and cherry trees stand in this spot. They look to be of great age and bear
nothing more than a few small wizened fruits now, yet their spacing suggests
that once they were much more lush and productive. But now this orchard has long
since fallen into old age and obscurity, just as the rest of Fangorn Forest has.
name: An Orchard in the Forest

desc:
Amid the other trees stands a massive horse chestnut. Its trunk would take five
fully grown men with arms outstretched to encircle and its massive branches
spread out over an area so large that it becomes an umbrella for many of the
trees around it. Conkers abound on the ground in such numbers as to be hazardous
to walk upon lest they roll aside causing one to lose their footing.
name: Under the Horse-Chestnut

desc:
Maybe the Ents and Huorns have let down their guard, or perhaps the smothering
moss, lichen, creepers and bindweeds have been encouraged to dominate this
particular area of the forest. Certainly, nothing seems to last long here
without being dragged down and throttled by bindweed and then covered utterly by
a thick mat of undergrowth.
name: The Smothering Plants

desc:
Sloping up from the Entwash north of here, a wooden wall of countless trees
rises out of the ground, blocking progress in all directions save west or onto
the river itself. Trees grow right up to the stone, but even they cannot gain
sufficient hold on the nearly vertical slopes to grow further up it. Mosses and
lichens do a better job however, slowly etching and eroding the surface as the
seasons pass.
name: A Great Wall

desc:
A giant tree stands here, dominant over the others. This mature yew grows, proud
and aloof, with needles so dense about it as to form an immeasurably thick
spongy carpet. Its massive bole dwarfs all the trees around it. A small outcrop
in the ground nearby yields a spring of fresh water, a feature that perhaps
helped this tree to outgrow it's neighbours to such an extent.
name: By the Giant Yew

desc:
Whether it was formed on a well-weathered outcrop of rock or a trunk of a
long-fallen tree it is now impossible to tell, but there is a notable clearing
here, in the middle of which is a mountainous shape covered in thick moss. It
must have laid undisturbed for countless years, because the moss itself is grey
and dusty, totally obscuring that which it lies on.
name: A Mountain of Moss

desc:
This seems to be part of the oldest areas of Fangorn Forest. It is packed with
densely grey trees, covered with thick coats of lichen and moss. The most
noticeable feature is a tall dark barrier of closely packed evergreen trees,
totally impenetrable from this position.
name: Against an Impenetrable Hedge

desc:
It is as if this place has been lost in time. The air is totally still and
rarely, if ever, is a sound heard. Tall smooth grey trunks stretch up to a broad
canopy high overhead. Twisted roots lie motionless and still, covering the
ground. Yet there is an atmosphere, not of emptiness and lifelessness, but
alertness; as if this area is being watched.
name: Tall Grey Trees and Tangled Roots

desc:
The Entwash curves from south to east, carving a path through the lush green
vegetation to either side. The open freshness of the area is invigorating
indeed, compared to the dim stuffiness of much of the forest. Dense plant-life
on both banks seem to thrive on the water's richness.
name: The Meandering Entwash

desc:
As the river glides smoothly from west to east it passes by an ancient coppice
of beech trees on the northern bank. Their roots twist thirstily into the
water's edge as their boughs spread out overhead to catch all the light and air
they can. One tree in particular has managed to grow a great branch almost
entirely across the river, so the Entwash slips under a natural arch as it
passes the trees.
name: Beneath the Beech Tree

desc:
The river is straight and calm along this stretch, with both banks sloping so
gently that it's impossible to tell where the reeds and bulrushes end and the
solid banks start. To the east the river continues, though there seems to be
something of a constriction of the waterway some distance downstream where the
land gets more rocky and uneven.
name: A Straight Stretch of the River

desc:
The river narrows and speeds up considerably as its way eastwards is blocked by
a cliff. It is forced to bend around to the south. The cliff wall itself is made
of a hard rock, though it is deeply cracked and worn, bearing deep underhangs
and niches formed by the slow eroding flow of the Entwash.
name: Curving around a Cliff

desc:
The Entwash river winds its way through hilly terrain, while changing its
general direction of flow from southerly to more easterly for a few miles. The
north-eastern bank is largely comprised of a rugged cliff which, although
heavily forested on top, means little vegetation reaches the water's edge on
that side. By contrast, to the west and south, large graceful stands of willow
trees stand, swaying in the air and reaching into the currents to quench their
ancient thirsts.
name: A Winding Curve on the Entwash

desc:
As the Entwash flows slowly along it deepens until the bottom is dark and
scarcely visible. The river narrows and less light reaches the surface as a line
of old mossy larches leans in threateningly from the eastern bank and a rank of
ancient and hoary elms looms menacingly from the west. Tentacle-like roots snake
into the water from both sides, writhing and snatching at anything that passes
close by. The banks are definitely out of bounds to travellers.
name: A Dark Stretch on the River

desc:
The river widens and brightens as it curves gently eastwards, the southern bank
sloping softly into a pleasant grassy sward with a single large boulder resting
at the water's edge. Meanwhile the river continues to flow lazily along,
becoming increasingly shallow as it does so.
name: A Gentle Curve in the River

desc:
The river seems to carve a divide between darkness and light. Ancient larches,
twisted and bent with writhing grasping roots and tendrils reach into the water
from the northern bank. One cannot leave the river on that side. Meanwhile a
proud cluster of silver birches and the occasional rowan tree stand tall on the
southern side, lifting each and every leaf to the fresh air above the Entwash.
name: A Straight Course on the River

desc:
As the river turns slowly from east to south once more, a large sandy bar
stretches into the water from the north bank, making it possible to wade almost
half-way across the river. The pebble-strewn riverbed can be seen through the
relatively clear water as if looking through a glass. In great contrast to this
peaceful spot, to the east a great darkness appears to descend on the forest as
if some great evil lives there.
name: In the Shallows

desc:
Though deeper here than to the north, the waters remain shallow. Yet the bottom,
previously sandy, has more silt to it now, muddying the clarity of the river.
Thick stands of reeds and bulrushes cluster on the south bank, making eerie
hollow scratching noises when the air passes through them, rattling their woody
stems against each other.
name: Among the Reeds

desc:
The northern bank is dominated by a black brake of larches and pines. There
seems to be a sense of looming hatred and malice, so much that the very waters
of the Entwash seem to shrink back from the earth on that side. The river
continues smoothly straight to the east where things seem somewhat brighter.
name: Beside a Black Brake

desc:
The forest of Fangorn stretches mile upon mile before it opens up into the
Plains of Rohan toward the southeast. The river Entwash passes the leagues in
its own slow and meandering fashion, seeming in no hurry to either leave the
forest or cross the broad plains before it meets and joins with the great River
Anduin in a wide delta, before finally turning to the distant sea.
name: Upon the Slow Entwash River

desc:
Although the Entwash flows serenely along just south of here, this area is
extremely dark and foreboding. Light seems to actively avoid this grove of dark
pines and larches. The river may be entered to the south, though a return up the
steep muddy bank appears to be impossible. However, southwards looks to be a
less dangerous direction than into the black forest north or west of here. A
great evil can be felt close by, and this area is not far from the heart of it.
name: A Dark Bank on the River

desc:
Upon entering this part of the forest, a rustling noise emanating from the edge
of vision can be heard. The low groaning sound seems to contain great anger for
whatever has disturbed this area. The trees jostle with grasping branches, and
seem to move in closer surrounding intruders. No escape may be had north or west
of here, and a deep ravine scores the land on the east, barring the way in that
direction also. The only path away from here lies to the south.
name: Almost Trapped among the Trees

desc:
The trees crowd and jostle one another and now and then a snide whispering can
be heard amongst the leaves and twigs. The floor is thickly matted with ancient
pine needles which muffle the air into a heavy and inert mass. To describe this
place as dangerous would not even be close to accurate.
name: Inside a Dark Pine Brake

desc:
The very air here throbs with malice. Even light and water, sacred to all
plant-life, shun the area. The source of the dread is not clear, but it is
probably ancient indeed. Yet it is clear that plants, as with other life, are
swayed by evil as well as good. Surely, this is one of the most hateful of
Fangorn's many dark groves.
name: The Heart of Evil

desc:
A dark brake of larches and pines has grown up in this part of the forest,
extending south and east into even darker areas. The air is thick and laden with
animosity and hatred as the evergreens rattle and creak ominously all around.
Many might find this reason enough to beat a hasty path west or north to lighter
areas of Fangorn.
name: Within a Black Brake

desc:
From west to east the ground becomes rocky and hilly, a steep slope pushing its
way eastwards. In all directions travel looks like it could be tough, especially
uphill, where the trees seem to have no regard for the rough terrain and just
grow tighter and tighter together. A bare outcrop of rock here yields a very
small spring.
name: On a Steep Slope

desc:
Northwards the pines and firs are pressed so tightly together that no progress
can be made, while east of here a deep cleft runs north to south as if a dragon
of an earlier age raked the ground asunder. There is no way down, and certainly
no way back up. Southwards, though the forest seems darker and more foreboding
passage is possible.
name: At the Edge of a Deep Cleft

desc:
There seems to be much more open land to the northwest while travel looks
progressively harder southeast of here. The ground becomes rocky and is crossed
by deep ridges, hard to climb down into and even tougher still to emerge from.
The trees seem to pack ever closer together on any land flat enough to support
them.
name: Uneven Ground

desc:
The trees suddenly give way to the west and south, where steep cliff faces
plummet thirty feet or more into the turbulent waters below. One could certainly
leap from here into the Entwash, although avoiding smashing onto rocks within
the fast-flowing river could prove difficult. The trees gather thickly to the
north and east as the land slopes away from the cliffs.
name: Overlooking the Entwash

desc:
Passage through the forest suddenly constricts to form a narrow corridor, where
the land slopes sharply downwards west to east, bordered by the Entwash to the
south and an impenetrable wall of branches to the north. What lies beyond that
wall is impossible to see from here. The boughs are so tightly interwoven
together that the traveller is forced either uphill to the west or downhill to
the east, unless a dangerous jump into the river is more appealing.
name: Along a Narrow Corridor

desc:
The trees seem to part slightly in this region, creating a clearer stretch of
land running from south to east of here. This not only allows for much easier
travelling in those directions, but the stuffiness and claustrophobia under
which so much of the forest lies, is almost totally absent.
name: A Breath of Fresh Air

desc:
The trees grow unevenly, thick to the south and east, yet widely spaced and
invitingly clear north and west. But in this area they are particularly thinly
spread, creating an elongated glade inhabited by grasses and ferns only. The air
is clear and fresh right to ground level, unusual indeed for Fangorn.
name: An Opening in the Forest

desc:
The plant life in Fangorn Forest is astounding in its richness and variety.
Despite the omnipresent stuffiness and age, with tendrils of moss and beards of
lichen hanging from every aged tree limb, there is a tangible vigour to the
place. Indeed, the undergrowth is thick and active, every plant striving for
more light than its neighbour. Even the trees have their own slow battle,
broadleaved oaks, ashes and beeches pushing against the needles of the firs,
pines and spruces.
name: Lush Undergrowth under a Mixed Canopy

desc:
The air is fresh and clear, despite the dense forest all around. The ground
slopes gently downhill from north to south, towards the Entwash. Indeed, a
slight sound of gurgling water can be heard. To the east things look decidedly
darker and more foreboding.
name: On a Gentle Slope

desc:
With the Entwash curving around so close by, the earth here is spongy and soft.
Yet the banks are still able to support a wild grove of larches, twisting
threateningly across the river, and blocking any passage to the water from this
area. Northwards there is some open space, a pleasant relief from this malignant
atmosphere.
name: Spongy Ground amid the Larches

desc:
Although no permanent path through Fangorn Forest can be found, there seems to
be a remarkably clear way through the trees in this area. It leads from the bank
of the Entwash on a heading north-eastwards into the forest. Certainly the way
north of here would be much easier going than any other direction. It is as if
the forest itself wants to guide travel that way.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
The current is strong and turbulent here as the water flows against a solid
cliff face to the west and the river is forced southwards around it. However,
despite the strong eddies, the water is clear so the rocky cliff and the
riverbed can be easily seen.
name: Underwater by a Cliff

desc:
The river's surging against the rock outside causes large and continuous waves
of sound to echo around this rocky void. As if that were not unsettling enough,
the roof is pierced through with tangled roots and the floor and walls are rough
with cracks and crevices. Anything could be lying in the dark, waiting to feast
on whatever creatures happen to stray in here.
name: Inside a Dark and Damp Cave

desc:
This is the back of the cave. The sound of the river is less loud here and the
air less moisture-laden. Yet tree roots seem to penetrate every crack from every
wall, giving many dark nooks and crannies. It seems that things are constantly
moving in peripheral vision. Who knows what is hiding and lurking in this dark
hole.
name: At the Back of the Cave

desc: You leap bravely from the cliff top and into the turbulent river below.
name: Jumping into the Entwash

desc: You launch yourself from the cliff top towards the rushing waters below.
name: Jumping into the River

desc: You leap boldly off the cliff and into the turbulent waters below.
name: Leaping into the Entwash

desc:
A change in the terrain of this area is echoed by a similar transition in the
Entwash river. To the west, where the land is hillier and rockier, the river is
fast and rough. However, despite a cliff making up the northern bank, the land
begins to flatten out as it goes south and east. The river, though still
rock-strewn and turbulent here, slows down considerably as it makes a southward
turn. The vegetation on either side is rich and dense, fed by the clear waters
of the Entwash.
name: Rough Water

desc:
The river straightens for a couple of miles, narrowed between a steep earthen
bank to the south and a rocky pitted cliff face to the north. With the rough
riverbed littered with worn boulders, the water leaps and surges as it careens
along, white water jostling and throwing around any river-travellers in an
alarming fashion.
name: A Series of Rapids
